steps.title: Identify the journalist's key claim
steps.content: The journalist states that quality signals like Oscar nominations can drive strong lifetime earnings even from a relatively modest opening weekend.

steps.title: Find the supporting data pair
steps.content: Choice B shows La Cage aux Folles (Oscar nominated) with a small opening weekend of just $18,709, yet it achieved lifetime earnings of $20,424,259—higher than Padmaavat (not nominated), which had a much larger opening weekend of $4,493,384 but only $11,846,060 in lifetime earnings. The Oscar nomination provided the quality signal that overcame weak initial interest to generate superior long-term success, directly supporting the journalist's claim. The other options either show a non-nominated film outperforming a nominated one, compare films with the same Oscar status, or provide unrelated data without demonstrating the claimed relationship. That matches choice B — B is correct.
